detaileddescription.para: This page provides detailed documentation for the various graph classes used in the bsm_4tops analysis. These classes inherit from graph_template and define the specific nodes, features, and topologies used to represent the event as a graph.

sect1.title: Graph of Tops
sect1.para: The graph_tops class constructs a graph where each node is a top quark.

listitem.para: graph_tops(): Initializes the graph name to "graph_tops".
graph_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the graph_tops graph.

void CompileEvent(): Defines the graph structure and features.
Nodes: The nodes of the graph are the top quarks from the event (event->Tops).
Graph Truth Features: signal, ntops.
Node Truth Features: top_node, res_node.
Edge Truth Features: res_edge.
Graph Data Features: met, phi, weight, event_number.
Node Data Features: pt, eta, phi, energy, charge.

sect1.title: Graph of Top Children
sect1.para: The graph_children class constructs a graph where each node is a decay product of a top quark.

listitem.para: graph_children(): Initializes the graph name to "graph_children".
graph_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the graph_children graph.

void CompileEvent(): Defines the graph structure and features.
Nodes: The nodes of the graph are the top decay products from the event (event->Children).
Topology: A fully connected topology is used.
Graph Truth Features: signal, n_nu, n_lep, ntops.
Node Truth Features: top_node, res_node.
Edge Truth Features: res_edge, top_edge.
Graph Data Features: met, phi, num_jets, num_leps, weight, event_number.
Node Data Features: pt, eta, phi, energy, charge, is_lep, is_b, is_nu.

sect1.title: Graph of Truth Jets
sect1.para: The graph_truthjets class constructs a graph using truth jets and leptonic top decay products.

listitem.para: graph_truthjets(): Initializes the graph name to "graph_truthjets".
graph_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the graph_truthjets graph.

void CompileEvent(): Defines the graph structure and features.
Nodes: The nodes are the truth jets and the leptonic/neutrino decay products of the top quarks.
Topology: A fully connected topology is used.
Features: Similar to graph_children, but with num_truthjets as a graph data feature.

sect1.title: Graph of Truth Jets (No Neutrinos)
sect1.para: The graph_truthjets_nonu class is similar to graph_truthjets, but excludes neutrinos from the graph nodes.

listitem.para:
void CompileEvent():
Nodes: The nodes are the truth jets and the leptonic (non-neutrino) decay products of the top quarks.
Neutrino Information: The double_neutrino() method is called to add information about the neutrinos to the graph, even though they are not included as nodes.

sect1.title: Graph of Reconstructed Jets
sect1.para: The graph_jets class constructs a graph using reconstructed jets and leptonic top decay products.

listitem.para: graph_jets(): Initializes the graph name to "graph_jets".
bool PreSelection(): A preselection method that can be used to filter events.

void CompileEvent(): Defines the graph structure and features.
Nodes: The nodes are the reconstructed jets and the leptonic/neutrino decay products of the top quarks.
Topology: A fully connected topology is used.
Features: Similar to graph_truthjets, but using reconstructed jets.

sect1.title: Graph of Reconstructed Jets (No Neutrinos)
sect1.para: The graph_jets_nonu class is similar to graph_jets, but excludes neutrinos from the graph nodes.

listitem.para:
void CompileEvent():
Nodes: The nodes are the reconstructed jets and the leptonic (non-neutrino) decay products of the top quarks.
Neutrino Information: The double_neutrino() method is called to add information about the neutrinos.

sect1.title: Graph of Detector Objects (with Neutrinos)
sect1.para: The graph_jets_detector_lep class constructs a graph using all detector-level objects (jets, electrons, muons) and truth neutrinos.

listitem.para:
void CompileEvent():
Nodes: Reconstructed jets, electrons, muons, and truth neutrinos.

sect1.title: Graph of Detector Objects (No Neutrinos)
sect1.para: The graph_detector class constructs a graph using all detector-level objects (jets, electrons, muons).

listitem.para:
void CompileEvent():
Nodes: Reconstructed jets, electrons, and muons.
Neutrino Information: The double_neutrino() method is called to add information about the neutrinos.
